marketState: Neutral-to-bearish with a near-term weather-driven bounce risk
summary: Price sits well below its seasonal median (-13.5% YTD vs -3.4% median) and seasonality odds skew down (only 31% chance higher in 4 weeks, typical move -3.1%), reinforced by a bearish storage surprise (+72 Bcf vs forecast, +184 Bcf above the 5y average) and a bearish EMA structure across every technical timeframe. Against that, 1-7 day weather demand is running +18.2% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range and rising fast, while 4h/1d technicals are oversold (RSI 26/36, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) - a setup for a short-lived counter-trend bounce even inside a heavier bear backdrop. Fair value pegs the front contract as essentially FAIR at every horizon (front spread +$0.03 now, widening only modestly to -$0.07 by +21D), so there is no large mispricing edge either way. Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, ratio 0.80) but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range, leaving room to extend shorts rather than signaling an extreme. Seasonality reliability is explicitly flagged Low (50pp spread), which caps conviction on the longer-term seasonal read.

longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, price is already -13.5% YTD vs a -3.4% seasonal median, and 4-week seasonality odds favor lower prices (31% higher, typical -3.1% move) - a structurally oversupplied backdrop into the Cooling season.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained storage deficit vs forecast, or 8-16D weather demand accelerating well above +5-10% vs 5y, would remove the oversupply thesis and invalidate the short.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D cooling demand is +18.2% vs 5y and near the top of its range (rangePos 0.98) and still rising (+24.5 24h), while 4h/1d technicals are oversold (RSI 26.2/35.9, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) - a near-term bounce setup even though the broader multi-timeframe EMA trend (50<200 everywhere) stays bearish.
shortTerm.invalidation: If 1-7D demand rolls over back toward the 5y norm or price closes below $2.85, the bounce thesis fails and the broader bearish trend resumes.

keyDrivers: Storage +184 Bcf vs 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build
Price -13.5% YTD vs -3.4% seasonal median; 4-week seasonality odds only 31% higher
1-7D cooling demand +18.2% vs 5y, rangePos 0.98 and rising fast, but 8-16D demand only +1.4% (fades)
Bearish EMA structure (50<200) on every technical timeframe, with 4h/1d oversold (RSI 26/36)
Managed money net short (-60,295, ratio 0.80) at only the 35th percentile of its 5y range
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly Low (50pp spread) - the seasonal edge could easily fail
Weather forecasts can shift quickly; the 1-7D demand spike could intensify or fade faster than modeled, whipsawing both trades
Fair value shows price as FAIR at every horizon, limiting the mispricing edge and making both trades sensitive to the next storage/weather data revision
